title: calceolaria corymbosa subsp. tetraphylla
intro: Calceolaria corymbosa subsp. tetraphylla, also known as the four-leaved slipper flower, is a flowering plant species native to South America. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Calceolaria corymbosa subsp. tetraphylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to produce a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
